content.full_text:
Farn Polrucel PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 24,2003
Betty Presley, Treasurer
Mimi Walters for State Assembly
Betty Presley & Associates, Inc.
30151 Tomas Street
Rancho Santa Margarita, CA 92688
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-03-226
Dear Ms. Presley:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Mimi Walters for State
Assanbly for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1
QUESTION
May a legislative candidate amend her Form 501 to change her designation
regarding the voluntary expenditure limits when the initial selection was made by
mistake?
CONCLUSION
No. The law provides only one instance under which a state candidate may
change the election or rejection of voluntary expenditure limits made on the candidate's
Form 501. That circumstance is not present here.
FACTS
At the time that Mimi walters's Form 501 was filed, the box reflecting
acceptance or rejection of the voluntary expenditure limits for the March 2,2004,
primary was elroneously check "yes," by your office, contrary to what Ms. Walters had
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18709-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-03-226
Page No. 2
instructed. The Form 501 was signed by Ms. Walters; however, she did not review the
document before she signed it. This elror was identified recently, and she desires to
amend the Form 501. To date, she has complied with the Proposition 34 contribution
limits for candidates who have accepted the voluntary expenditure lirnits.
Our response to your request for advice was withheld at your request pending
consultation with the campaign's attorney. On November 20,2003, your attorney
communicated to this office the campaign's desire for a written response.
ANALYSIS
Proposition 34, adopted by the voters in November of 2000, established a
comprehensive contribution limit scherne and other provisions to the Act allowing
candidates for elective state office, such as the AssemblS to accept voluntary expenditure
limits. ($$ 85300 - 85321; 85400-85403.) Together these sections provide an overall
framework that seeks to limit the comrpting influence of large donations and supports a
system that reduces the need for large camfaign war chests. As originally enacted by
Proposition 34, section 85401 requires each candidate to file a statement of accqltance or
rejection of the voluntary expenditure limits when the statement of intention is filed.
($ 85401, subd. (a).)
Once the Form 501 is filed, section 85401 speaks to the amendment of that form
to alter the election or rejection of expenditure limits and provides just a single
circumstance for amendment :
"(b) Any candidate for elective state office who declined to accept
the voluntary expenditure limits but who nevertheless does not
exceed the limits in the primary, special primary, or special election,
may file a statement of acceptance of the expenditure limits for a
general or special runoffelection within 14 days following the
primary, special primary, or special election.,'
The FPPC has advised on numerous occasions that, except for the limited
circumstance described in subdivision (b) above, a candidate may not amend his or her
Form 501. (Daniels-Meade Advice Letter, No. A-01-285; Boling Advice Letter, No. A-
01-136.) Commission staff reiterated this conclusion in the Daniels-Meade Advice
Letter, No. A-03-178, when the Office of the Secretary of State inquired whether this
advice applied in the context of the election for Governor held on Octobef 7 , 2OO3 . ln
that letter, the Secretary of State's office was adyised that "[b]ecause nothing in the Act
would allow a candidate to amend the expenditure limit designation in connection with a
recall election," the amended filing of the Form 501 should be returned to the candidate.
(Daniels-Meade Advice Letter, No. ,{-03-178, atp. 3.) Most recently, this advice was
confirmed in the Sutton Advice Letter,No. A-03-253. A copy of that letter and its
thorough legal analysis supporting the Commission's interpretation is enclosed.


File No. A-03-226
Page No. 3
Because the circumstances of section 85401, subdivision (b), are not met, Ms.
Walters maynot amend her Form 501 to change the expenditure limit designation.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
(
By:
C.
Tocher
Legal Division
Enclosure
CSTjg

sections.question:
May a legislative candidate amend her Form 501 to change her designation
regarding the voluntary expenditure limits when the initial selection was made by
mistake?

No. The law provides only one instance under which a state candidate may
change the election or rejection of voluntary expenditure limits made on the candidate's
Form 501. That circumstance is not present here.

QUESTION: May a legislative candidate amend her Form 501 to change her designation
regarding the voluntary expenditure limits when the initial selection was made by
mistake?

CONCLUSION: No. The law provides only one instance under which a state candidate may
change the election or rejection of voluntary expenditure limits made on the candidate's
Form 501. That circumstance is not present here.
